context: The bald eagle is not rough. The bear does not need the bald eagle. The dog needs the bear. If someone is rough then they chase the bald eagle. If someone needs the bear then they are not blue. If the bear is nice then the bear chases the bald eagle. If someone eats the bald eagle then they are rough. If someone is cold and they chase the bear then the bear is rough. If someone eats the bear and they need the bear then the bear does not eat the bald eagle. If the dog is cold and the dog is nice then the dog eats the bald eagle. If someone needs the bear then they eat the bald eagle.
label: True
formalisations:
PREDICATES:
needs(x, y) ::: x needs y
rough(x) ::: x is rough
bald_eagle(x) ::: x is a bald eagle
bear(x) ::: x is a bear
dog(x) ::: x is a dog
chases(x, y) ::: x chases y
blue(x) ::: x is blue
nice(x) ::: x is nice
eats(x, y) ::: x eats y

PREMISES:
NOT rough(bald_eagle)
NOT needs(bear, bald_eagle)
needs(dog, bear)
forall x (rough(x) -> chases(x, bald_eagle))
forall x (needs(x, bear) -> NOT blue(x))
forall x (nice(bear) -> chases(bear, bald_eagle))
forall x (eats(x, bald_eagle) -> rough(x))
forall x forall y (cold(x) and chases(x, bear) -> rough(bear))
forall x forall y (eats(x, bear) and needs(x, bear) -> NOT eats(bear, bald_eagle))
forall x (cold(dog) and nice(dog) -> eats(dog, bald_eagle))
forall x (needs(x, bear) -> eats(x, bald_eagle))

CONCLUSION:
needs(dog, bear)

context: The bear is not cold. The lion does not need the squirrel. The mouse likes the squirrel. The squirrel sees the lion. If someone is round and young then they see the bear. If someone is round and they do not need the squirrel then the squirrel likes the mouse. If someone needs the squirrel and they are cold then they see the mouse. If someone sees the squirrel and the squirrel does not like the lion then they need the bear.

young(x) ::: x is young
cold(x) ::: x is cold
bear(x) ::: x is a bear
lion(x) ::: x is a lion
mouse(x) ::: x is a mouse
squirrel(x) ::: x is a squirrel

NOT needs(lion, squirrel)
likes(mouse, squirrel)
sees(squirrel, lion)

forall x (likes(x, squirrel) and needs(x, lion) -> likes(x, mouse))

forall x (round(x) and NOT needs(x, squirrel) -> likes(squirrel, mouse))
forall x (needs(x, squirrel) and cold(x) -> sees(x, mouse))
forall x (sees(x, squirrel) and NOT likes(squirrel, lion) -> needs(x, bear))

CONCLUSION:
sees(squirrel, lion)

If someone needs the tiger and they do not visit the tiger then the tiger is not nice. If the bear visits the bald eagle then the bald eagle needs the bear. context: Anne is furry. Anne is green. Anne is rough. Gary is furry. Gary is not kind. Gary is red. Gary is rough. Harry is not furry. Harry is red. Harry is rough. If someone is green then they are furry. All rough people are not kind. If someone is kind and furry then they are rough. If Harry is green and Harry is kind then Harry is not furry. If someone is rough and not kind then they are not cold. If Harry is cold and Harry is rough then Harry is young.
label: True

green(x) ::: x is green
rough(x) ::: x is rough

cold(x) ::: x is cold
young(x) ::: x is young

green(anne)
rough(anne)
furry(gary)
NOT kind(gary)
red(gary)
rough(gary)
NOT furry(harry)
red(harry)
rough(harry)
forall x (green(x) -> furry(x))
forall x (rough(x) -> NOT kind(x))
forall x (kind(x) AND furry(x) -> rough(x))
forall x (green(x) AND kind(x) -> NOT furry(x))
forall x (rough(x) AND NOT kind(x) -> NOT cold(x))
forall x (cold(x) AND rough(x) -> young(x))

CONCLUSION:
red(harry)

context: Bob is quiet. Harry is rough. If someone is red then they are rough. Kind people are furry. If someone is kind then they are nice. All red people are kind. If Harry is furry then Harry is not nice. If Bob is not kind and Bob is not rough then Bob is not quiet.
label: True
formalisations:
PREDICATES:
quiet(x) ::: x is quiet
rough(x) ::: x is rough
red(x) ::: x is red
kind(x) ::: x is kind
furry(x) ::: x is furry
nice(x) ::: x is nice

PREMISES:
quiet(bob)
rough(harry)
forall x (red(x) -> rough(x))
forall x (kind(x) -> furry(x))
forall x (kind(x) -> nice(x))
forall x (red(x) -> kind(x))
forall x (furry(x) -> NOT nice(x))
forall x (NOT kind(x) AND NOT rough(x) -> NOT quiet(x))

CONCLUSION:
rough(harry)
